sections.recommendation:
Following a positive recommendation from the Police Accountability Board at their
February 5, 2025 regular meeting, adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to
execute a contract and any necessary amendments with Edgeworth Integration LLC for
the purchase and installation of a new security camera system server (cameras already
exist) to replace an obsolete server at Berkeley Animal Care Services (BACS) for
March 12, 2025, to March 11, 2026, which establishes a contract in an amount not to
exceed $57,908.08.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
This security camera system is a critical need for Berkeley Animal Care Services. The
total contract cost will be covered with existing budget funds for BACS.
sections.current_situation:
BACS has been without a working security camera system for over 2 years due to an
unsupported/unavailable browser. This contract will enable BACS to install new
software so the existing security camera system can be utilized again. The system is
vital as it allows us to identify people who abandon animals at the shelter. It is also
essential for safeguarding our facility, staff, volunteers, and animals when the shelter is
open and after hours.
sections.background:
The City’s Animal Shelter has had an existing interior and exterior security camera
system for years, but the footage from this system has become inaccessible due to an
unsupported server. The City has established previous contracts with Edgeworth
Integrations for security camera systems at the Police Jail, Transfer Station, and Marina
facilities. Staff recommend a sole source waiver for this contract because Edgeworth
Integration is the only retailer authorized by the Avigilon brand for sale and installation
of its Fixed Camera Video Surveillance System equipment. Avigilon Fixed Camera
Video Surveillance Systems are already in use at several locations throughout the City:
 In the Jail

 At the intersection of 6th & University
 At the Marina Waterfront
Other manufacturer brands of surveillance equipment were initially considered in 2018
when the City Council authorized purchase and installation of a Fixed Camera Video
Surveillance System for San Pablo Park, but none provided the same functionality and
reliability as the Avigilon line of Video Surveillance equipment. Therefore the City
contracted with Edgeworth Integration for purchase and installation of an Avigilon Fixed
Camera Video Surveillance System in San Pablo Park, and then for each subsequent
installation location to date.
Pursuant to Berkeley Municipal Code 2.99.030, Neighborhood Services sent copies of
the proposed Use Policy and Acquisition report for the server to the Director of Police
Accountability requesting that the Police Accountability Board (PAB) review and
consider these reports within 30 days. The PAB agendized these reports at their
February 5, 2025 regu
sections.rationale:
Entering a contract with Edgeworth Integration LLC would allow BACS to better
provide safety to our staff, volunteers, and animals. BACS already has an existing
system of internal and external security cameras, but the security camera footage has
become inaccessible due to obsolete software. In the time since that footage has
become inaccessible to staff, the animal shelter has seen numerous concerning
incidents, including a physical assault on a staff member, routine illegal dumping of
live animals at the shelter front door during overnight hours, theft of animals from the
shelter kennels, and more. Allowing the shelter to once again use the existing and
previously operable security camera system provides an additional way to document
and rectify inappropriate or dangerous behavior from animal owners or members of
the public. If the action is not approved, BACS cannot identify and cite those who
abandon animals at our facility, continuing to add to the shelter’s highest intake
numbers since 2017. If not approved, BACS will continue to be unable to ensure the
safety of our animals overnight, and assaults against staff members may be difficult to
prosecute. In the past, it has been necessary to have a restraining order issued
against individuals, and the lack of cameras to document the behavior of owners or
other members of the public at our facility reduces our ability to safeguard our staff
and volunteers and weakens our case if needed to pursue legal actions.

ATTACHMENT 1
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: EDGEWORTH INTEGRATION LLC TO PURCHASE AND PROVIDE
INSTALLATION OF A SECURITY SYSTEM AT BERKELEY ANIMAL CARE SERVICES
WHEREAS, security camera system is a critical need for Berkeley Animal Care
Services (BACS), essential for safeguarding the facility, staff, volunteers, and animals;
and
WHEREAS, BACS must ensure that our animals are safe overnight while no staff are
present; and
WHEREAS, a security camera system assists staff in documenting issues arising from
upset owners or members of the public; and
WHEREAS, it is necessary to address and hopefully prevent members of the public
from abandoning animals at the shelter; and
WHEREAS, Edgeworth Integrations LLC has contracted with the City to provide security
camera system equipment at other existing City facilities; and
WHEREAS, Edgeworth Integrations LLC submitted a reasonable quote to purchase and
provide installation of a security camera system at the Dona Spring Animal Shelter; and
WHEREAS, the Police Accountability Board, at their February 5, 2025 regular meeting,
reviewed and voted to approve copies of the surveillance technology acquisition report
and use policy governing use of this technology, in accordance with Berkeley Municipal
Code 2.99.030; and
WHEREAS, the City Council finds that the benefits to community and animal safety and
welfare of installing this technology, used according to its Surveillance Use Policy,
outweigh the costs; that the proposal will appropriately safeguard civil liberties and civil
rights to the maximum extent possible while serving its intended purposes; and that, in
the City Council’s judgment, no feas
